title: aloysia herrerae
intro: Aloysia herrerae, also known as the Mexican tea tree, is a plant species native to Mexico and Central America, belonging to the Verbenaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Aloysia herrerae include relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate digestive problems. Aloysia herrerae can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and it is also used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including skin conditions and wounds.
